description: Beta/Prod Deployment, CI/CD Refactor, and UX Consistency Fixes - Remove friends requirement from Burn Squad creation, provision beta environment, split CI/CD workflows, and add post-deployment tests

userStories.title: Update Bicep to support beta environment
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Bicep to accept 'beta' as a valid environment so I can provision beta infrastructure.
acceptanceCriteria: infra/main.bicep @allowed constraint updated to ['dev', 'beta', 'prod']
infra/main.beta.bicepparam exists with environment = 'beta'
Beta param file references 'buddyburn-beta-kv' as Key Vault name and 'buddyburn-beta' as resource group
Beta param file uses 'burnbuddyacr.azurecr.io' as container registry server
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove friends requirement from Burn Squad creation
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create a Burn Squad without having friends added yet, so I can set up my squad and invite people later.
acceptanceCriteria: When user has no friends, Burn Squad creation page shows informational message ('No friends to invite yet — you can add them later') but does NOT block form submission
Create Burn Squad button remains enabled when a squad name is entered, regardless of friend selection
A squad created with zero inviteUids is accepted by the API and returns 201
User is redirected to the home page after creating an empty squad
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create API deploy workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow for the API so that only API-relevant changes trigger API builds and deploys.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/deploy-api.yml exists and triggers on pushes to main when services/api/**, packages/shared/**, or infra/** change
Workflow includes quality checks (typecheck + API tests) before building
Workflow builds only the API Docker image and deploys only the API Container App
Workflow deploys to beta first, then production, in sequence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Create web deploy workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow for the web app so that only web-relevant changes trigger web builds and deploys.
acceptanceCriteria: .github/workflows/deploy-web.yml exists and triggers on pushes to main when apps/web/** or packages/shared/** change
Workflow includes quality checks (typecheck) before building
Workflow builds the web Docker image with correct build args (Firebase config, API URL) for each environment
Workflow deploys to beta first, then production, in sequence
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Remove old deploy workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the original monolithic deploy.yml removed so it does not cause duplicate runs alongside the new split workflows.
acceptanceCriteria: Original .github/workflows/deploy.yml is removed or renamed to deploy.yml.disabled
No duplicate workflow runs occur when pushing to main
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Only remove after deploy-api.yml and deploy-web.yml are created

userStories.title: Create API integration test script
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an integration test script that verifies live API endpoints so regressions are caught after deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Test script exists (e.g. scripts/integration-test.sh or tests/integration/) and accepts BASE_URL environment variable
Tests cover: GET /health (200), POST /users (401 without token), GET /users/me (401), GET /friends (401), POST /workouts/start (401)
Script exits non-zero on any test failure
Test results are clearly formatted for CI output
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: az CLI is authenticated — use `az keyvault secret show` to fetch Firebase test user credentials from Key Vault at runtime for authenticated endpoint tests.

userStories.title: Add Playwright E2E setup and browser tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Playwright browser tests for critical user flows so the web app is verified after each deployment.
acceptanceCriteria: Playwright is added as a dev dependency
E2E tests cover: load home/login page (200, no JS errors), navigate to Burn Squads page, create a new Burn Squad with no friends
Tests accept BASE_URL as environment variable to target beta or production
Playwright configured with retries: 1 for CI flakiness tolerance
Typecheck passes for any TypeScript test files
userStories.notes: Use `az keyvault secret show` to fetch test account credentials from Azure Key Vault at runtime. az CLI is authenticated.

userStories.title: Wire API integration tests into deploy workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests to run automatically after each API deployment so failures are caught immediately.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy-api.yml runs integration tests against beta after beta deploy
deploy-api.yml runs integration tests against prod after prod deploy
If integration tests fail, the workflow job fails (no silent failures)
Test results are visible in GitHub Actions summary
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on deploy-api.yml (US-003) and integration test script (US-006)

userStories.title: Wire E2E tests into web deploy workflow
userStories.description: As a developer, I want E2E browser tests to run after each web deployment so critical user flows are verified in both environments.
acceptanceCriteria: deploy-web.yml runs Playwright E2E tests against beta after beta deploy
deploy-web.yml runs Playwright E2E tests against prod after prod deploy
Test artifacts (screenshots, traces) are uploaded as GitHub Actions artifacts on failure
If E2E tests fail, the workflow job fails
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on deploy-web.yml (US-004) and Playwright tests (US-007)

userStories.title: Provision beta Azure environment
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the beta Azure infrastructure provisioned so automated deploys have a live target environment.
acceptanceCriteria: buddyburn-beta resource group exists in Azure
buddyburn-beta-kv Key Vault exists with required secrets mirrored from prod
Beta Container Apps environment (buddyburn-beta-env) is provisioned via Bicep
Beta API Container App (buddyburn-beta-api) is reachable and returns 200 on GET /health
Beta Web Container App (buddyburn-beta-web) is reachable and returns 200
userStories.notes: az CLI is authenticated — use `az deployment group create`, `az keyvault secret set`, and `az containerapp` commands directly to provision and verify resources.

userStories.title: Deploy and verify API pipeline end-to-end
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the API deploy workflow to successfully push a Docker image and update beta and production Container Apps.
acceptanceCriteria: API workflow runs without errors on push to main
Docker image is built, tagged with short commit SHA, and pushed to burnbuddyacr
buddyburn-beta-api Container App is updated to the new image
buddyburn-prod-api Container App is updated to the new image
All workflow steps show green in GitHub Actions UI
userStories.notes: az CLI is authenticated — use `az containerapp show`, `az acr login`, and `gh workflow run` to iterate. No manual intervention needed.

userStories.title: Deploy and verify web pipeline end-to-end
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the web deploy workflow to successfully push a Docker image and update beta and production web Container Apps.
acceptanceCriteria: Web workflow runs without errors on push to main
Docker image for apps/web is built with correct build args for each environment
buddyburn-beta-web Container App is updated to the new image
buddyburn-prod-web Container App is updated to the new image
All workflow steps show green in GitHub Actions UI
userStories.notes: az CLI is authenticated — use `az containerapp show`, `az acr login`, and `gh workflow run` to iterate. No manual intervention needed.
